---
toc: true
bibliography: ../../references.bib
csl: ../../american-medical-association-brackets.csl
---

# Proteomics

Last Revised: July 24, 2025  
VERSION 2.1

## Background

Serum and plasma samples were processed and stored at the University of North
Texas Health Science Center Institute for Translational Research and were run
across three waves (Wave 1: 2019; Wave 2: 2021; Wave 3: 2022). Blood samples
were initially prepared through an automated system utilizing a customized
Hamilton Robotics StarPlus system. Proteomic assay kits were purchased from
select vendors (Meso Scale Discovery, Quanterix). Samples were run in
singlicate, with a subset run in duplicate. Control samples were included across
plates and runs for quality control.

## Methods

*Electrochemiluminescence (ECL)*

Proteomic assay kits were commercially obtained from Meso Scale Discovery (MSD;
<a href="http://www.mesoscale.com"
target="_blank">http://www.mesoscale.com</a>). Per our previously published
methods,[@petersen2020proteomicA; @petersen2020proteomicB] samples were assayed
using electrochemiluminescence (ECL) technology. The ECL platform has been
extensively applied to assay biomarkers associated with the range of
neurodegenerative diseases including Alzheimer’s disease (AD). The UNTHSC
laboratory maintains a database of over n>2000 samples from the neurotypical AD
population conducted in duplicate using the ECL technology on the referenced MSD
platform with average coefficient of variation (CVs) &lt; 10%. A total of 500ul
of plasma and serum were assayed for the following proteomic markers: fatty acid
binding protein 3 (FABP3), beta 2 microblogulin (B2M), Factor VII, I-309,
pancreatic polypeptide (PPY), c-reactive protein (CRP), thrombopoietin (TPO),
interleukin (IL)-5, IL-6, IL-8, IL-10, IL-18, alpha 2 macroglobulin (A2M),
eotaxin 3, vascular cell adhesion molecule-1 (sVCAM-1), soluble intercellular
adhesion molecule-1 (sICAM-1), thymus and activation regulated chemokine (TARC),
tenascin C, tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-a), and serum amyloid A (SAA) (see
Table 1). Five pooled plasma and serum control samples were included on each MSD
plate layout reflecting a similar distribution of age for Down Syndrome (DS)
samples and sibling controls.

*Single Molecular Array (SIMOA)*

Proteomic assay kits were commercially obtained from Quanterix 
(<a href="http://quanterix.com" target="_blank">http://quanterix.com</a>). A
total of 500 ul of plasma was assayed using Single Molecule Array (SIMOA)
technology using HD-1 imager for some of Wave 1 assays and HD-X imager for Wave
1, Wave 2, and 3 (see Table 2). The following proteomic markers were derived:
Amyloid beta (Aβ) 40 and 42, total tau, ptau181, glial fibrillary acidic protein
(GFAP) and neurofilament light chain (NfL). Five pooled plasma control samples
were included on each SIMOA plate layout reflecting a similar distribution of
age for DS samples and sibling controls.

## QA/QC

All supplies for key biological resources were purchased from multiple vendors
via commercially available kits (e.g. Meso Scale Discovery; Quanterix). Each of
these vendors conducts internal validation of resources. Protocols are conducted
to ensure reproducibility of findings. Protocols include validation of
resources, which spans monitoring dilution linearity, spiked recovery,
monitoring of low and high end of detection ranges to avoid lack of reliability
at restricted ranges, examination of matrix effects, inclusion of pooled
standards across lots, examination of inter- and intra-assay variability,
real-time monitoring of coefficient of variation (CVs) and monitoring of all lot
numbers across experiments.

**Table 1. Lot Number and Assay Information (Meso Scale Discovery)**

<div class="btn-group mb-3">
  <strong>Wave:</strong>
  <button class="wave-btn active" data-wave="Wave 1" selected>Wave 1</button>
  <button class="wave-btn" data-wave="Wave 2">Wave 2</button>
  <button class="wave-btn" data-wave="Wave 3">Wave 3</button>
</div>

<div class="btn-group mb-3">
  <strong>Sample:</strong>
  <button class="type-btn active" data-type="Plasma" selected>Plasma</button>
  <button class="type-btn" data-type="Serum">Serum</button>
</div>

<div id="variable-table"></div>

<br>

**Table 2. Lot Number and Assay Information (Quanterix)**

<div class="btn-group mb-3">
  <strong>Wave:</strong>
  <button class="wave-btn-2 active" data-wave="Wave 1" selected>Wave 1</button>
  <button class="wave-btn-2" data-wave="Wave 2">Wave 2</button>
  <button class="wave-btn-2" data-wave="Wave 3">Wave 3</button>
  <button class="wave-btn-2" data-wave="Wave 4">Wave 4</button>
</div>

<div id="variable-table2"></div>

<script src="js/proteomics.js"></script>

<br>

**Error Coding**: -777777 Missing; -999999 LLDL

## Updates to Previous Release

<table class="table">
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="width:15%">Column Name</th>
<th style="width:15%">Date Effective</th>
<th style="width:50%">Reason/Information</th>
<th style="width:20%">Action</th>
</tr>
</thead>

<tbody>
<tr>
<td>Project</td>
<td>4/25/2025</td>
<td>Internal identifier</td>
<td>Removed from dataset</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td>BatchName</td>
<td>4/25/2025</td>
<td>Internal identifier</td>
<td>Removed from dataset</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td>Conc_Merged</td>
<td>4/25/2025</td>
<td>Combined into a single column. From wave 4 onwards, use Comment_30%CVflag to identify if singlet or duplicate data.</td>
<td>Removed from dataset</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td>Comment_30%CVflag</td>
<td>4/25/2025</td>
<td>Information on interpretation is available in the cell comments.</td>
<td>Removed from dataset</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td>Comment_Instrument</td>
<td>4/25/2025</td>
<td>Data error from instrument</td>
<td>Removed from dataset</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
